history.subject: Quot base change: pullback finite presentation and flatness closed (Stacks 01BK/01U9). pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite proved via per-layer Presentation.map/ofIsIso IsFinite instances (new Picard/PullbackFinitePresentation.lean, carved from QuotFunctorDef); CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback proved by assembling flat_section_pullback_piece + flat_section_of_affine_cover (new Picard/QuotFlatBaseChange.lean). QuotFunctorDef down to 3 sorries; blueprint lem:pullback_finite_presentation/lem:coherent_flat_base_change proofs now \leanok (flatness proof rewritten to the formalized affine-local route).
Albanese: land Milne 3.3 substep 1 — difference rational map Φ = f(x)·f(y)⁻¹
Albanese: prove isIntegral_pullback_self — X ×_k̄ X integral for smooth geom-irred X
Milne 3.3 substep 1: Dom(Φ) ⊇ Dom(f)×_k̄ Dom(f) (le_domain_differenceRationalMap), axiom-clean

Milne 3.3 sub-step 2 algebraic content: reconstruction f(x)=Φ(x,u)·f(u) as domain bound Dom(Φ)⊓pr₂⁻¹(Dom f)≤Dom(f∘pr₁), via group identity (a·b⁻¹)·b=a transported through Over.forget; plus reusable le_domain_precomp and le_domain_prod bricks
blueprint: match lem:rationalMap_prod_domain_bound hypotheses to Lean (S/Y/Z separated, sY/sZ locally of finite type); drop toPartialMap identifier leak in substep-1 proof (adversarial-verify fixes)

Close the three Lane-F flat-base-change leaves: the canonical mate $g^* f_* \mathcal F \to f'_* g'^{*} \mathcal F$ is an isomorphism on sections over every open of $S'$ (qcqs $f$, flat $g$, quasi-coherent $\mathcal F$). The Beck–Chevalley elementwise compatibility of the mate with the adjunction-unit base maps (KEY-BC) is proved outright axiom-clean; general opens reduce to affine pairs by basis locality. The single remaining typed leaf is the algebraic Stacks 02KE $H^0$ heart (finite-affine-cover equalizer + flat tensor exactness), with two of its bricks already proved.

Prove proper-support base change axiom-clean and assemble the Hilbert-function base-change invariance: for a cartesian square and finitely presented $\mathcal F$ with support proper over $S$, the support of $g'^*\mathcal F$ is proper over $S'$ (annihilators pull back via the smul-endomorphism transport along pullback ⊣ pushforward + affine localization vanishing, Stacks 056H/01PC/01W4; new file QuotSupportBaseChange.lean, zero sorries). The fibrewise Hilbert function $m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(t)} \Gamma((X_T)_t, \mathcal F_t \otimes L_t^{\otimes m})$ is invariant under base change of $T$, assembled from the fibre-pasting cartesian square modulo two named leaves (pullback/tensor-power monoidality, Stacks 01CD; $\Gamma$-base-change over the residue extension, Stacks 02KH i=0). The Quot functor's base-change action is now fully well-defined.

The relative-divisor functor is a real functor (Kleiman §3): $\mathrm{Div}(T)$ = families $\mathcal O_{X_T} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal O_D$, $\mathcal O_D$ flat over $T$ with proper support and invertible kernel ideal, modulo $\ker q = \ker q'$, base-changed through the Quot engine (new file DivFunctorDef.lean; one typed leaf: invertibility of the pulled-back kernel ideal, by $\mathrm{Tor}_1$-vanishing from $T$-flatness). In the FGA assembly, divFunctor is rewired to the real functor (instHasDivFunctor proved) and the Picard-scheme existential is strengthened to carry local finiteness (Kleiman th:main(1)), so instPicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType is proved — the FGA file's sorries drop 4 → 2 (representability itself and the Abel map).
